# services.sslh

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.sslh`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.sslh.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable sslh, protocol demultiplexer. |
| `services.sslh.listenAddresses` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | Listening addresses or hostnames. |
| `services.sslh.method` | `one of "fork", "select", "ev"` | The method to use for handling connections: - `fork` forks a new process for each incoming connection. It is well-tested and very reliable, but incurs the overhead of many processes. - `select` uses only one thread, which monitors all connections at once. It has lower overhead per connection, but if it stops, you'll lose all connections. - `ev` is implemented using libev, it's similar to `select` but scales better to a large number of connections. |
| `services.sslh.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Listening port. |
| `services.sslh.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (libconfig value)` | sslh configuration. See {manpage}`sslh(8)` for available settings. |
| `services.sslh.settings.numeric` | `boolean` | Whether to disable reverse DNS lookups, thus keeping IP address literals in the log. |
| `services.sslh.settings.protocols` | `list of attribute set of (libconfig value)` | List of protocols sslh will probe for and redirect. Each protocol entry consists of: - `name`: name of the probe. - `service`: libwrap service name (see {manpage}`hosts_access(5)`), - `host`, `port`: where to connect when this probe succeeds, - `log_level`: to log incoming connections, - `transparent`: proxy this protocol transparently, - etc. See the documentation for all options, including probe-specific ones. |
| `services.sslh.settings.timeout` | `unsigned integer, meaning >=0` | Timeout in seconds. |
| `services.sslh.settings.transparent` | `boolean` | Whether the services behind sslh (Apache, sshd and so on) will see the external IP and ports as if the external world connected directly to them. |
| `services.sslh.settings.verbose-connections` | `integer between 0 and 4 (both inclusive)` | Where to log connections information. Possible values are: 0. don't log anything 1. write log to stdout 2. write log to syslog 3. write log to both stdout and syslog 4. write to a log file ({option}`sslh.settings.logfile`) |
